questions.question: What does Uncle Bob mean when he says 'saying I'll try is lying'?
questions.expectedAnswer: If you say 'I'll try' to meet a deadline, you are implying you have been holding back effort. Either you can commit to the deadline or you cannot. Saying 'I'll try' avoids the honest conversation about what is realistic. Professionals give honest estimates and say 'no' when necessary.
questions.rubric: Must explain the dishonesty of 'I'll try' and the obligation to be honest

questions.question: How does Uncle Bob respond to the criticism that TDD is dead?
questions.expectedAnswer: Uncle Bob acknowledges that TDD done badly can harm design, but argues that TDD done well improves design. The problem is not TDD itself but the skill with which it is applied. TDD is a discipline that requires practice and judgment, not mechanical application.
questions.rubric: Must show understanding of both the criticism and Uncle Bob's nuanced response

questions.question: What is the first item in the Programmer's Oath?
questions.expectedAnswer: I will not produce harmful code. This establishes that software developers have an ethical obligation to the users and public who depend on their software.
questions.rubric: Must state the first oath item and connect it to ethical obligation
